target_runtime: kimi-code CLI is the canonical target for this eval (the marketplace is published via the 4 plugin manifests and consumed by Claude Code / Codex / Cursor / Kimi Code; kimi-code is the most directly addressable from this session)

kimi.transcript_format: stream-json (newline-delimited JSON events: message, tool_use, tool_result, etc.)

jsonl_capture.correction_reason: Initially assumed JSONL capture was unavailable because kimi-code does not expose its own session log programmatically. Verified by running `kimi --help` and `claude --help` — both CLIs support --output-format stream-json, which emits parseable NDJSON of the full session. --skills-dir on kimi is the key capability for with/without comparison.

browser.note: vision-via-mcp only; no HTML rendering for review reports

method.grading: Parse each NDJSON stream. For each, list the (a) Read tool calls (which SKILL.md files were consulted?) and (b) the final response text. Behavioral signal: with-skill run that reads the EXPECTED skill's SKILL.md AND applies its guidance = routing win. with-skill run that doesn't read any skill or reads a wrong one = routing loss.
